rules.statement: CLI commands MUST follow 'resource action [flags]' pattern (depth=2), except admin namespace which may use depth=3.
rules.rationale: Resource-first design maps to user mental models and prevents architecture leakage into UX.

rules.statement: Resource names MUST NOT be 'mind', 'body', 'will', 'manage', 'check', or 'fix' as these expose internal architecture.
rules.rationale: Internal architecture layers should not be visible in user-facing commands.

rules.statement: Action names SHOULD use standard verbs from the approved architectural vocabulary.
rules.rationale: Consistent vocabulary improves discoverability and reduces cognitive load.

rules.statement: Commands that mutate state MUST require --write flag or interactive confirmation.
rules.rationale: Prevents accidental data loss and provides audit trail for mutations.

rules.statement: All async commands MUST be wrapped with @core_command decorator to handle event loop execution.
rules.rationale: Ensures proper async/await handling and prevents RuntimeWarning errors.

rules.statement: CLI command and resource modules MUST resolve at process start using static imports; import failures MUST be fatal (no error-swallowing around CLI module imports, no deferred or lazy loading that masks load errors).
rules.rationale: A fail-fast import surface guarantees every registered command is functional at startup. Enforced structurally by the absence of error-swallowing around the CLI loader, not by a discovery loader. Restated per ADR-076 D6: the prior statement described a CORE_DEV_MODE discovery loader that was never implemented anywhere in the codebase; the rule now matches the observed, enforceable mechanism.

rules.statement: Every command MUST provide help text and usage examples.
rules.rationale: Self-documenting CLI improves usability and reduces support burden.
